page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is an x86_64 architecture memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon processor. It features 80 vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 1922.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 24.02 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a clear trade-off between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a Passmark CPU score of 39,241.27 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 41.72 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is stable, reaching 123.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though the overall Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a high-density memory allocation profile. It is designed for workloads that require massive RAM capacity rather than high single-threaded CPU speed. The server is suited for large in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and caching layers like Redis, where it achieves an average rating of 40.16 million operations per second. It is less suitable for compute-bound tasks or LLM text generation, where performance is poor.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon processor and 1922.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.2 GHz across 40 physical cores with hyper-threading, it provides a substantial memory-to-vCPU ratio of 24.02 GB. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct trade-off: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks consistently perform in the average tier. Qualitatively, this server offers high memory density, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory footprints rather than high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized server with 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring massive RAM capacity.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity for memory-bound enterprise workloads and large-scale in-memory databases.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 24.02 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and caching layers
